Kayenta Township FY2022 Audit/Knowledge Base

Kayenta Township — FY2022 Audit

Project Knowledge File

Fiscal Year: October 1, 2021 – September 30, 2022
Audit Firm: Snyder & Brown, CPAs, PLLC (Tempe, AZ)
Engagement Partner: Robert Snyder
Audit Seniors: Kyle Tevis and Anthony Carbone
Prior Year Auditor: Heinfeld, Meech & Co., P.C. (Scottsdale, AZ)
Client Consultant: Steve Ward <steve@wardneal.com>
Knowledge File Last Updated: June 10, 2026 (v1.1)

PURPOSE: Persistent knowledge base for the Kayenta Township FY2022 audit engagement. Seeded into the project so all conversations have context about the entity, accounting structure, trial balance model, prior year results, current year data, and engagement planning details.

AUDIT SCOPE: General Fund (Fund 100) and Capital Projects Fund (Funds 205, 300, 303). No single audit required. Solid Waste Enterprise Fund (Fund 501) — confirm scope with engagement team. Government-wide layer (Funds 800, 900) — set aside per planning meeting but will need conversion AJEs.

v1.1 CHANGELOG (June 10, 2026): Updated to reflect clarified source-file mapping and expanded GL data. (1) TB and GL column headers renamed to their final, clarified labels: Fund Num, Act Code, Dept Num, GL Num. (2) Account-number mapping logic rewritten to the confirmed FFF-A-DD-OOOO convention (Section 2.3). (3) Activity Code (the "A" segment) definitions refined against actual TB data. (4) GL detail section (Section 6) expanded to cover three years — 2021, 2022, and 2023 — now available as project files. (5) Corrected the prior (erroneous) GL column interpretation. All dollar amounts/balances are unchanged from v1.0; only the structural/mapping documentation was revised.

SECTION 0 — ENTITY OVERVIEW AND KEY FACTS

  • Established: 1996, by resolution of the Navajo Nation Council
  • Status: Home-rule municipality on Navajo Nation (Arizona), graduated from Kayenta Township Pilot Project in 2003
  • Governing body: 5-member Board of Commissioners (separately elected)
  • Town Manager (PY): Mrs. Gerri Laughter
  • Town Manager (CY per draft): Mr. Jarvis Williams
  • Mailing address: P.O. Box 1490, Kayenta, AZ 86033
  • Component units: None
  • Key revenue sources: Retail business sales tax (ordinance 1997), business site leasing (ordinance 1999), hotel occupancy tax, junk food tax (appears discontinued CY), lease rentals via Navajo Nation (3% admin fee), intergovernmental grants
  • Business-type activity: Solid Waste Transfer Station (enterprise fund)
  • Tourism significance: Monument Valley — international tourist destination; COVID-19 significantly impacted hotel occupancy taxes and related revenues in FY2020-2021; recovery evident in FY2022 (+150% HOT increase)
  • Insurance: Through Navajo Nation Risk Management Program (including workers' compensation)
  • PY opinion: Unmodified (clean) on governmental activities, business-type activities, and each major fund
  • PY report date: February 21, 2025 (significant delay from FY2021 year-end)
  • PY findings: FS-2021-001 (Capital Assets — MW); FS-2021-002 (Accounting Records — MW)
  • Accounting department: 3 positions authorized, only 2 filled as of PY year-end

SECTION 1 — ENGAGEMENT OVERVIEW

1.1 Planning Meeting Summary (June 9, 2026)

  • Client: Kayenta Township — new engagement for Snyder & Brown
  • Team: Kyle Tevis (Senior), Anthony Carbone (Senior), Robert Snyder (Partner)
  • Year-end: September 30, 2022
  • Funds in scope: General Fund and Capital Projects Fund only
  • Single audit: Not required per client
  • Entity-wide layer: Set aside for now
  • Client consultant: Steve Ward (steve@wardneal.com) — primary audit contact
  • Prior CPA: Heinfeld, Meech & Co., P.C.
  • Approach: Use prior CPA's groupings and lead sheets; convert into engagement-friendly format
  • GL access: Cannot be accessed directly by the client
  • Controls documentation: Begin only after binder and account structure are organized

1.2 Key Action Items

  1. Kyle to set up engagement binder using pre-existing groupings
  2. Kyle to import and group the trial balance and tie to draft FS
  3. Hold off on controls documentation until binder is set up
  4. Route all client questions through Bob (Robert Snyder)
  5. Confirm engagement letter retention in audit binder
  6. Evaluate whether GF and Capital Projects scope is complete
  7. Determine if entity-wide adjustments will be needed later
  8. Document rationale for no single audit

SECTION 2 — TRIAL BALANCE MODEL

2.1 TB File Structure

File: Keyenta_Township_TB_with_Group_and_Subgroup_2022_and_2021.xlsx (typo "Keyenta" in filename)

Three tabs: (1) Trial Balance 2022 and 2021 (primary, 11 cols, 364 data rows), (2) 2022 with ADJ (14 cols), (3) 2021 with ADJ (14 cols).

Primary Tab Columns (final, clarified labels): Account #, Fund Num, Act Code, Dept Num, GL Num, Description, Fund, Group, Subgroup, 9/30/2022, 9/30/2021

Adjustment Tab columns: Account #, Fund Num, Act Code, Dept Num, GL Num, Description, Fund, Group, Subgroup, [Year] Unadj, AJEs, [Year] Adj, RJEs, [Year] Report

Header note: The four segment columns (Fund Num, Act Code, Dept Num, GL Num) are the broken-out components of the full account string in column "Account #". See Section 2.3 for how they concatenate.

Convention: Revenue negative (credit), expenditures positive (debit). TB balances to zero by fund (verified).

2.2 Fund Codes

CodeNameTypeScope
100General FundGovernmentalIN SCOPE
205Capital Project FundGovernmentalIN SCOPE
300Capital Project FundGovernmentalIN SCOPE
303Capital Project FundGovernmentalIN SCOPE
501Solid Waste Enterprise FundProprietaryCONFIRM
800GFAAG - Fixed AssetsGW ConversionCONVERSION
900GLTDAG - Long Term DebtGW ConversionCONVERSION

2.3 Account Number Structure (Mapping Logic)

The full account string in the Account # column is built from four broken-out segments:

Fund Num – Act Code – Dept Num – GL Num

Format: FFF-A-DD-OOOO

SegmentWidthColumn headerMeaning
FFF3Fund NumFund Number (see Section 2.2)
A1Act CodeActivity Code (sub-fund / activity layer — see table below)
DD2Dept NumDepartment Number (see Section 2.4)
OOOO4GL NumGL Object Code (account type — see ranges below)

Example: 100-1-00-1012 = Fund 100 (General Fund), Activity 1 (primary), Dept 00 (general/balance sheet), Object 1012 (Wells Fargo – Primary checking).

Activity Code (the "A" segment)

The Activity Code distinguishes activity layers within a fund. Confirmed values from the TB:

ActMeaningFund(s)Typical content
1Primary / main operating activity100, 205, 300, 303, 501, 800, 900Cash, taxes, core payroll, standard fund operations; default layer for every fund
2Special programs & designated activities100, 300F100 Dept 19: community events, 4th of July (rodeo/pow wow/song & dance/skate), scholarships, library, law-enforcement support, donations. F300 Dept 01: designated capital improvements (Vendor Village/Rodeo Grounds, Homesite Improvements, HWY 160 Drainage, building repairs)
3Capital projects — infrastructure300Dept 98: chip-seal roads (Cottonwood Dr), streetlights, industrial park gravel road, post office road improvement
4Enterprise / Solid Waste501, 900SW 5-yr CD, AR – solid waste fees, buildings, improvements other than buildings; enterprise long-term items mirrored in F900
7Recreation Center / facilities100Dept 20: building R&M, equipment & furniture
9Fund 205 capital project activity205Broadband grant, due to other funds, reserved fund balance, transfers in from General Fund

GL Object Code ranges (the "OOOO" segment)

RangeAccount class# accounts
1xxxAssets43
2xxxLiabilities24
3xxxEquity / Fund Balance4
4xxxRevenue24
5xxxExpenditures (operating)64
6xxxDepreciation / Interest7
7xxxCapital Outlay19

2.4 Department Codes

DeptFunctionNotes
00General/Admin/RevenueB/S, revenue, payroll liabilities
01Admin/OperationsAdmin expenditures, transfers, SW ops; F300 Act 2 designated improvements
10Board of CommissionersStipends, travel
11Community DevelopmentSalaries, benefits — significantly decreased CY
13Finance/AccountingFinance staff, audit fees, consulting
14IT/TechnologyNew CY — zero PY activity
15Town Manager/AdminTM salaries, attorney, legal
17Public WorksMassive CY growth (dept restructuring from 11)
19Community Events/Grants4th of July, scholarships, library (F100 Act 2)
20Facilities/CustodialCustodial staff, janitorial, rec center (F100 Act 7)
21Fire Dept/Public Safety (VFD)Kayenta VFD salaries, equipment
98Capital Projects InfrastructureStreetlights, industrial park (Fund 300 Act 3)

2.5 Group/Subgroup Mapping

55 unique Group values. No Subgroup values populated. Groups map directly to FS line items.

Key revenue groups: Sales Taxes, Hotel Occupancy Tax, Lease Rentals, Junk Food Tax (zero CY), Construction taxes, Licenses and Permits, Charges For Services, Penalties and Interest, Other Income, Unrestricted Investment Income, Grants and Contracts Income, Transfers In

Key expenditure groups: General Government, Community Development, Public Works, Public Safety, Culture and Recreation, Highways and Streets, Transfers out, Capital Outlay, Personnel Services, Fringe Benefits, Depreciation Expense

Cross-segment note: Group is assigned at the account level and does not map 1:1 to Activity Code. For example, F100 Act 2 (Dept 19) accounts carry mixed Groups — most are Culture and Recreation, but several are General Government (library, donations, veterans) or Public Safety (law-enforcement support). Always roll up to FS lines by the Group field, not by Act Code.

SECTION 3 — PRIOR YEAR RESULTS (FY2021)

3.1 Government-Wide

  • Total net position: $30,082,933 (Gov $29,852,154 + BTA $230,779)
  • Change in net position: $1,170,531 (Gov +$1,470,202 / BTA -$299,671)

3.2 Governmental Funds

  • GF fund balance: $14,873,805 (unassigned)
  • Capital Projects fund balance: $(819,984) (deficit)
  • Total governmental fund balances: $14,053,821
  • GF revenue: $3,715,616 / GF expenditures: $2,264,224

3.3 Proprietary Fund

  • SW net position: $230,779
  • Operating loss: $(293,141)
  • Change in net position: $(299,671)

3.4 Prior Year Findings

FS-2021-001 — Capital Assets (MW): CY activity not on GL, depreciation rollforward errors, formula errors in schedules. Root cause: only 2 of 3 accounting positions filled.

FS-2021-002 — Accounting Records (MW): $79,350 / $4,866 unsubstantiated payroll liabilities, $577,492 grant receivable not reversed. Audit significantly delayed. Root cause: consultant transition, business office turnover.

SECTION 4 — PRIOR YEAR ADJUSTING ENTRIES

4.1 Government-Wide AJEs

  • GW03: Reclassify enterprise fund activity ($1.1M)
  • GW04: Capital asset additions ($2.5M — infrastructure, equipment, CIP)
  • GW05: Depreciation by function ($1.5M total)
  • GW07: BNP adjustment for PY revenue ($9,658) — non-recurring
  • GW10: Reclassify GF revenues ($567K/$606K)
  • GW12: Net position — invested in capital assets ($16.2M)
  • GW17: Expense non-capitalized capital outlay ($854K)
  • GW19: Reclassify capital projects revenues ($759K)
  • GW35: Environmental remediation payment ($16,580)

4.2 Fund-Level AJEs

  • GF 101: Compensated absences accrual ($26,954) — recurring
  • GF 107: Interfund negative cash elimination ($430,164) — recurring
  • CP 302: Interfund borrowing ($102,924) — recurring
  • CP 303: Grant receivable reversal ($577,492) — non-recurring
  • SW 503: Interfund borrowing ($327,240) — recurring
  • SW 505: Compensated absences change ($316) — recurring
  • SW 508: Net position reclassification ($581K) — recurring
  • SW 513: Capital lease principal + interest ($58K) — COMPLETED (lease paid off CY)
  • SW 522: Capital assets + depreciation ($98K) — recurring

SECTION 5 — CURRENT YEAR PRELIMINARY ANALYSIS

5.1 Key Balance Movements

  • Cash up 31% (revenue growth)
  • HOT receivable up 298% — investigate
  • Lease Receivable $382,629 — new (GASB 87)
  • Interfund restructured: Due From eliminated, transfers instead
  • HOT revenue +150% (COVID recovery)
  • Junk Food Tax -100% (discontinued?)
  • Dept 17/11 expenditure shift (PW restructuring)
  • Capital lease paid off
  • SW interfund borrowing repaid/restructured

5.2 Draft FS Key Totals

  • Government-wide net position: $31,107,107
  • GF fund balance: $15,153,362
  • GF revenue: $4,235,144 / expenditures: $2,919,929
  • SW net position: $595,179
  • Beginning net position increased $306,212.17 vs FY2021 audited closing (NOT GASB 87-related — see Section 8.6). Verified components: 800-1-00-3005 imported opening +$92,187.49 (FS-2021-001 accum. depr. matter); 100-1-00-3020 imported opening +$83,632.32; Fund 900 layer opening +$1,644.31 (FY21 AJE 1 comp-absence reclass not reflected in import); 303-1-01-3020 client AJE +$128,748.05 (posted in TB, support needed)

5.3 Revenue Analysis (GF)

Sales Taxes: $3,243,355 (+18.4%), HOT: $619,475 (+150%), Lease Rentals: $425,748 (-17%), Junk Food Tax: $0 (-100%), Total GF Revenue: $4,410,169 (+18.7%)

5.4 Expenditure Analysis (GF)

Gen Gov: $1,043,135 (-14%), Public Safety: $364K (+8%), Public Works: $613K (+8,000%), Community Dev: $121K (-82%), Transfers Out: $1,121K (new)

SECTION 6 — DETAILED GENERAL LEDGER FILES (PBC)

Three years of detailed GL are now available as project files, all sharing the same 19-column layout and the clarified FFF-A-DD-OOOO account convention. Each file has a single worksheet named "General Ledger {year}".

6.1 Column Layout (all three years, identical — 19 columns)

#ColumnNotes
1Account #Full FFF-A-DD-OOOO string
2Fund NumFund segment (FFF)
3Act CodeActivity segment (A)
4Dept NumDepartment segment (DD)
5GL NumGL Object Code segment (OOOO)
6DescriptionAccount description
7FundFund name
8GroupFS grouping
9Subgroup(generally blank)
10SessionIDPosting batch/session
11DocNumDocument number
12DocDateTransaction/posting date
13DocDescDocument description
14NameVendor/payee/source
15TransDescTransaction description
16TypeD (debit) / C (credit)
17DebitDebit amount
18CreditCredit amount
19AmountSigned net amount

Correction (v1.1): The v1.0 column note misidentified columns 2 and 5 (previously shown as "GrantNum(FundCode)" and "FundNum(GLAcct)"). The correct segment columns are Fund Num (col 2), Act Code (col 3), Dept Num (col 4), GL Num (col 5).

Observations: (1) Conversion-fund (800/900) postings appear only in the 2021 GL; 2022/2023 GLs are fund-accounting only, so the government-wide layer must be built via conversion AJEs. (2) The 2022 GL shows minimal capital-projects activity (F300 only 17 txns) versus 2021 (215) and 2023 (183) — confirm whether FY2022 capital activity is captured here or sits in a separate project ledger. (3) DocDate ranges overlap fiscal years because of cash-allocation entries and accrual cutoffs; filter on DocDate for true period testing.

7.1 Capital Assets (Fund 800)

  • CIP: $586,741
  • Depreciable assets gross: $26,807,677 (infra $8.5M, airport infra $10.8M, land improvements $2.7M, buildings $1.8M, vehicles $2.0M, equipment $1.0M, signs $69K)

SECTION 8 — SPECIAL ACCOUNTING AREAS

8.1 Environmental Remediation

Origin 2014, UST removal at Hwy 160. Original estimate $728,141. CY balance $346,807. Evaluated annually.

8.2 Capital Lease

PY $52,271 (SW sanitation vehicles). CY $0 — PAID OFF.

8.3 GASB 87 / Leases

New CY: Lease Receivable $382,629 / Deferred Inflows $382,629. (The $306,212.17 beginning net position change is unrelated to GASB 87 — see Section 8.6.) Navajo Nation pass-through (3% admin fee) + direct Township leases (3-99 yr terms). PY future minimum rentals: $2,883,096.

8.4 Investments

US Gov Securities (GF): $4,131,733 (Level 1). CDs (SW): $48,140 (Level 1). Custodian: BNY Mellon. Collateralized beyond FDIC $250K.

8.5 401(k) Plan

Safe Harbor match 100% up to 4%. Immediate vesting. PY expense $18,638.

8.6 Beginning-Equity Bridge — VERIFIED (FY2021 Audited FS → FY2022 TB)

Workpaper: Kayenta_2021_FS_to_TB_and_2022_Rollforward_Check.xlsx. All figures from tab 1 ("Trial Balance 2022 and 2021"), col J (9/30/2022 report) and col K (9/30/2021 report), of the primary TB file.

Tie-out: FY2021 audited FS TIE to TB col K — total net position variance $(0.03); governmental $(0.66); business-type +$0.63. GF ties after the $26,954 compensated-absence classification (carried in Fund 900 acct 900-1-00-2230); agreed per FY_21 reconciliation schedule Note 1. All FY2021 audit adjustments are baked into col K (only two offsetting ±$4,304.12 P&L reclass differences vs the FY21 schedule; zero net position effect).

Rollforward: Tab 1 col J = tab "2022 with ADJ" Report column across all 364 accounts (zero differences). Both TB columns net to zero; every fund self-balances. FY2022 beginning equity exceeds FY2021 audited closing by exactly $306,212.17:

  • +$92,187.49 — 800-1-00-3005 imported FY2022 opening above FY2021 closing (no entry booked; = FS-2021-001 beginning accumulated-depreciation matter)
  • +$83,632.32 — 100-1-00-3020 imported FY2022 opening above FY2021 audited/agreed closing (no entry booked)
  • +$1,644.31 — Fund 900 layer opening (FY21 AJE 1 compensated-absence reclass not reflected in the import)
  • +$128,748.05 — discrete FY2022 client AJE to 303-1-01-3020 (posted in TB AJE column; 303 opening itself agreed to FY21 close)

Net RJE effect on total beginning equity: $0 (annual conversion rebuild only reallocates between funds/layers).

Key finding: The $306K is NOT in the FY2021 balances. Per the FY_21 reconciliation schedule "Reconciliation of Beginning Equity," the client's 10/1/2021 opening equity (e.g., 100-1-00-3020 at $(14,900,756.57)) originally AGREED to audited with zero adjustment. The difference arose from changes to the imported FY2022 opening balances made after that agreement, plus the FY2022 Fund 303 AJE. Remaining work is documentation, not reconciliation: obtain the client report/entries supporting the revised openings and the 303 AJE support, then evaluate GASB 100 presentation/disclosure for the $306,212.17.

8.6 Interfund

CY restructured — prior negative cash positions resolved via transfers instead of due to/from.
